[[尾崎洵盛]]（娘婿） | 国旗 = JPN | 職名 = 第14代 [[法務大臣|司法大臣]] | 内閣 = [[第1次西園寺内閣]] | 就任日 = [[1908年]][[3月25日]] | 退任日 = 1908年[[7月14日]] | 国旗2 = 東京都 | 職名2 = 第17代 [[東京都知事一覧|東京府知事]] | 就任日2 = [[1898年]][[11月12日]] | 退任日2 = 1908年3月25日 | 国旗3 = 静岡県 | 職名3 = 第4代 [[静岡県知事]] | 就任日3 = [[1897年]][[4月7日]] | 退任日3 = 1898年[[7月16日]] | 国旗4 = 埼玉県 | 職名4 = 第4代 [[埼玉県知事]] | 就任日4 = [[1894年]][[1月20日]] | 退任日4 = 1897年4月7日 | 国旗5 = JPN | 職名5 = [[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院議員]] | 選挙区5 = [[貴族院男爵議員]] | 当選回数5 = 4回 | 就任日5 = [[1890年]][[7月10日]] - 1897年[[7月9日]]
1897年7月10日 - [[1904年]]7月9日
1904年7月10日 - [[1911年]]7月9日
1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = 千家尊福 Senge Takatomi 2.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]].{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} By that he was an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}.https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. [[ja:千家尊福]][[simple:Takatomi Senge]] == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911 | before = {{ill|Gengaku Mutaguchi|ja|牟田口元学}} | after = Abolished }} {{S-other}} {{Succession box | title = Head of [[Izumo-taishakyo]] | years = [[1882]] - [[1888]] | before = Position established | after = [[千家尊愛]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲大社|出雲大社宮司]] | years = [[1872年]] - 1882年 | before = [[千家尊澄]] | after = [[千家尊紀]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲国造]]（千家家） | years = 第80代：1872年 - 1882年 | before = 千家尊澄 | after = 千家尊紀 }} {{S-reg|jp}} {{Succession box | title = 男爵 | years = [[出雲国造|千家家]]初代

1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]].{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} By that he was an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}.https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. 1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]].{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} By that he was an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}.https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|Mokuyōkai}} a Japanese left wing association == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. [[ja:千家尊福]][[simple:Takatomi Senge]] == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911 | before = {{ill|Gengaku Mutaguchi|ja|牟田口元学}} | after = Abolished }} {{S-other}} {{Succession box | title = Head of [[Izumo-taishakyo]] | years = [[1882]] - [[1888]] | before = Position established | after = [[千家尊愛]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲大社|出雲大社宮司]] | years = [[1872年]] - 1882年 | before = [[千家尊澄]] | after = [[千家尊紀]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲国造]]（千家家） | years = 第80代：1872年 - 1882年 | before = 千家尊澄 | after = 千家尊紀 }} {{S-reg|jp}} {{Succession box | title = 男爵 | years = [[出雲国造|千家家]]初代

1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]].{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} By that he was an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}.https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|Mokuyōkai}} a Japanese left wing association == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. 1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908 and in the [[House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|Mokuyōkai}} a Japanese left wing political party. The party was sympathetic to the [[Japanese Communist Party]] and [[Junpei Gomikawa]] published through it.{{Cite journal |last=Ward |first=Vanessa B. |date=2006-09-01 |title=The spectre of the left: Iwanami shoten, ideology and publishing in early postwar Japan |url=https://doi.org/10.1080/10371390600883578 |journal=Japanese Studies |volume=26 |issue=2 |pages=171–184 |doi=10.1080/10371390600883578 |issn=1037-1397 |s2cid=143914456}} == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. 1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|Mokuyōkai}} a Japanese left wing political party. The party was sympathetic to the [[Japanese Communist Party]] and [[Junpei Gomikawa]] published through it.{{Cite journal |last=Ward |first=Vanessa B. |date=2006-09-01 |title=The spectre of the left: Iwanami shoten, ideology and publishing in early postwar Japan |url=https://doi.org/10.1080/10371390600883578 |journal=Japanese Studies |volume=26 |issue=2 |pages=171–184 |doi=10.1080/10371390600883578 |issn=1037-1397 |s2cid=143914456}} == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. [[ja:千家尊福]][[simple:Takatomi Senge]] == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911 | before = {{ill|Gengaku Mutaguchi|ja|牟田口元学}} | after = Abolished }} {{S-other}} {{Succession box | title = Head of [[Izumo-taishakyo]] | years = [[1882]] - [[1888]] | before = Position established | after = [[千家尊愛]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲大社|出雲大社宮司]] | years = [[1872年]] - 1882年 | before = [[千家尊澄]] | after = [[千家尊紀]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲国造]]（千家家） | years = 第80代：1872年 - 1882年 | before = 千家尊澄 | after = 千家尊紀 }} {{S-reg|jp}} {{Succession box | title = 男爵 | years = [[出雲国造|千家家]]初代

He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. [[ja:千家尊福]][[simple:Takatomi Senge]] == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911 | before = {{ill|Gengaku Mutaguchi|ja|牟田口元学}} | after = Abolished }} {{S-other}} {{Succession box | title = Head of [[Izumo-taishakyo]] | years = [[1882]] - [[1888]] | before = Position established | after = [[千家尊愛]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲大社|出雲大社宮司]] | years = [[1872年]] - 1882年 | before = [[千家尊澄]] | after = [[千家尊紀]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲国造]]（千家家） | years = 第80代：1872年 - 1882年 | before = 千家尊澄 | after = 千家尊紀 }} {{S-reg|jp}} {{Succession box | title = 男爵 | years = [[出雲国造|千家家]]初代

{{Draft}} {{Afc comment|1=[[Takatomi Senge]] should link here[[User:FloridaArmy|FloridaArmy]] [[User_talk:FloridaArmy|talk]] 14:00, 27 May 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | 人名 = 千家 尊福 | 各国語表記 = せんげ たかとみ | 画像 = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg | 画像サイズ = 240px | 画像説明 = [[大礼服]]で[[正装]]した千家 | 国略称 = {{JPN}} | 生年月日 = [[1845年]][[9月7日]]
（[[弘化]]2年[[8月6日 (旧暦)|8月6日]]） | 出生地 = {{JPN}} [[出雲国]]
（現・[[島根県]]） | 没年月日 = {{death date|1918|1|3|1845|9|7}} | 前職 = [[神道大社教]]管長 | 所属政党 = （[[研究会 (貴族院)|研究会]]→）
[[木曜会 (貴族院)|木曜会]] | 称号・勲章 = [[正二位]]
[[勲一等旭日大綬章]]
[[男爵]] | 子女 = [[千家元麿]]（長男・[[庶子]]）
千家鯱丸（三男・庶子）
[[千家哲麿]]（六男） | 親族（政治家） = [[伏原宣明]]（義祖父）
[[千家尊澄]]（父）
[[千家尊統]]（婿養子）
[[千家紀彦]]（孫）
[[千家国麿]]（玄孫）
[[尾崎洵盛]]（娘婿） | 国旗 = JPN | 職名 = 第14代 [[法務大臣|司法大臣]] | 内閣 = [[第1次西園寺内閣]] | 就任日 = [[1908年]][[3月25日]] | 退任日 = 1908年[[7月14日]] | 国旗2 = 東京都 | 職名2 = 第17代 [[東京都知事一覧|東京府知事]] | 就任日2 = [[1898年]][[11月12日]] | 退任日2 = 1908年3月25日 | 国旗3 = 静岡県 | 職名3 = 第4代 [[静岡県知事]] | 就任日3 = [[1897年]][[4月7日]] | 退任日3 = 1898年[[7月16日]] | 国旗4 = 埼玉県 | 職名4 = 第4代 [[埼玉県知事]] | 就任日4 = [[1894年]][[1月20日]] | 退任日4 = 1897年4月7日 | 国旗5 = JPN | 職名5 = [[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院議員]] | 選挙区5 = [[貴族院男爵議員]] | 当選回数5 = 4回 | 就任日5 = [[1890年]][[7月10日]] - 1897年[[7月9日]]
1897年7月10日 - [[1904年]]7月9日
1904年7月10日 - [[1911年]]7月9日
1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|[[Mokuyōkai]]}} a Japanese left wing political party. The party was sympathetic to the [[Japanese Communist Party]] and [[Junpei Gomikawa]] published through it.{{Cite journal |last=Ward |first=Vanessa B. |date=2006-09-01 |title=The spectre of the left: Iwanami shoten, ideology and publishing in early postwar Japan |url=https://doi.org/10.1080/10371390600883578 |journal=Japanese Studies |volume=26 |issue=2 |pages=171–184 |doi=10.1080/10371390600883578 |issn=1037-1397 |s2cid=143914456}} == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. [[ja:千家尊福]][[simple:Takatomi Senge]] ==Izumo Taisha== {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} ==Administrator's family== The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}. After the separation those two families took the position of Izumo Kokuso by turns until the late 19th century. Shinto was reconstructed as modernized Japan's national religion in the late 19th century. In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. Senge and Kitajima established their religious corporations respectively, {{Nihongo|Izumo-taisha-kyo|出雲大社教}} by Senge and {{Nihongo|Izumo-kyo|出雲教}} by Kitajima. Under the [[Occupation of Japan|Allied occupation after World War II]], Shinto was separated from the government control and Izumo-taisha was reformed into a private shrine, then Senge and its Izumo-taisha-kyo took back the position of the administrator of Izumo-taisha. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. Currently, the position of the administrator of Izumo-taisha is succeeded by Senge lineage. Its Izumo-taisha-kyo is better known nationwide and has more followers in total, "{{Nihongo2|出雲大社 千家 尊統}} (1998/8)", but locally Kitajima lineage and its Izumo-kyo has more followers around Izumo region. Kitajima is the more orthodox Izumo kokuso. "{{Nihongo2|出雲国造系統伝略 北島斉孝}} (1898)". On October 5, 2014, Kunimaro Senge, eldest son of the current administrator Takamasa Senge, married [[Princess Noriko]] at the shrine. Princess Noriko is a daughter of the late Prince Takamado, a cousin of the now-[[Akihito|Emperor Emeritus of Japan]]."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911 | before = {{ill|Gengaku Mutaguchi|ja|牟田口元学}} | after = Abolished }} {{S-other}} {{Succession box | title = Head of [[Izumo-taishakyo]] | years = [[1882]] - [[1888]] | before = Position established | after = [[千家尊愛]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲大社|出雲大社宮司]] | years = [[1872年]] - 1882年 | before = [[千家尊澄]] | after = [[千家尊紀]] }} {{Succession box | title = [[出雲国造]]（千家家） | years = 第80代：1872年 - 1882年 | before = 千家尊澄 | after = 千家尊紀 }} {{S-reg|jp}} {{Succession box | title = 男爵 | years = [[出雲国造|千家家]]初代
[[1884年]] - [[1918年]] | before = （叙爵） | after = [[千家尊統]] }} {{End}}

He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|[[Mokuyōkai]]}} a Japanese left wing political party. The party was sympathetic to the [[Japanese Communist Party]] and [[Junpei Gomikawa]] published through it.{{Cite journal |last=Ward |first=Vanessa B. |date=2006-09-01 |title=The spectre of the left: Iwanami shoten, ideology and publishing in early postwar Japan |url=https://doi.org/10.1080/10371390600883578 |journal=Japanese Studies |volume=26 |issue=2 |pages=171–184 |doi=10.1080/10371390600883578 |issn=1037-1397 |s2cid=143914456}} == Political offices == Senge held many political offices in his life. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. [[ja:千家尊福]][[simple:Takatomi Senge]] ==Family== {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}. After the separation those two families took the position of Izumo Kokuso by turns until the late 19th century. Shinto was reconstructed as modernized Japan's national religion in the late 19th century. In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. Senge and Kitajima established their religious corporations respectively, {{Nihongo|Izumo-taisha-kyo|出雲大社教}} by Senge and {{Nihongo|Izumo-kyo|出雲教}} by Kitajima. Under the [[Occupation of Japan|Allied occupation after World War II]], Shinto was separated from the government control and Izumo-taisha was reformed into a private shrine, then Senge and its Izumo-taisha-kyo took back the position of the administrator of Izumo-taisha. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. Currently, the position of the administrator of Izumo-taisha is succeeded by Senge lineage. Its Izumo-taisha-kyo is better known nationwide and has more followers in total, "{{Nihongo2|出雲大社 千家 尊統}} (1998/8)", but locally Kitajima lineage and its Izumo-kyo has more followers around Izumo region. Kitajima is the more orthodox Izumo kokuso. "{{Nihongo2|出雲国造系統伝略 北島斉孝}} (1898)". On October 5, 2014, Kunimaro Senge, eldest son of the current administrator Takamasa Senge, married [[Princess Noriko]] at the shrine. Princess Noriko is a daughter of the late Prince Takamado, a cousin of the now-[[Akihito|Emperor Emeritus of Japan]]."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. 1897年7月10日 - [[1904年]]7月9日
1904年7月10日 - [[1911年]]7月9日
1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|[[Mokuyōkai]]}} a Japanese left wing political party. The party was sympathetic to the [[Japanese Communist Party]] and [[Junpei Gomikawa]] published through it.{{Cite journal |last=Ward |first=Vanessa B. |date=2006-09-01 |title=The spectre of the left: Iwanami shoten, ideology and publishing in early postwar Japan |url=https://doi.org/10.1080/10371390600883578 |journal=Japanese Studies |volume=26 |issue=2 |pages=171–184 |doi=10.1080/10371390600883578 |issn=1037-1397 |s2cid=143914456}} == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 After the separation those two families took the position of Izumo Kokuso by turns until the late 19th century. Shinto was reconstructed as modernized Japan's national religion in the late 19th century. In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. Senge and Kitajima established their religious corporations respectively, {{Nihongo|Izumo-taisha-kyo|出雲大社教}} by Senge and {{Nihongo|Izumo-kyo|出雲教}} by Kitajima. Under the [[Occupation of Japan|Allied occupation after World War II]], Shinto was separated from the government control and Izumo-taisha was reformed into a private shrine, then Senge and its Izumo-taisha-kyo took back the position of the administrator of Izumo-taisha. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. Takamasa Senge, a descendant of Takatomi, married [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]] at [[Izumo-taisha]] in 2014. Princess Noriko is a daughter of the late [[Norihito, Prince Takamado|Prince Takamado]], a cousin of the now-[[Akihito|Emperor Emeritus of Japan]]. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|[[Mokuyōkai]]}} a Japanese left wing political party. The party was sympathetic to the [[Japanese Communist Party]] and [[Junpei Gomikawa]] published through it.{{Cite journal |last=Ward |first=Vanessa B. |date=2006-09-01 |title=The spectre of the left: Iwanami shoten, ideology and publishing in early postwar Japan |url=https://doi.org/10.1080/10371390600883578 |journal=Japanese Studies |volume=26 |issue=2 |pages=171–184 |doi=10.1080/10371390600883578 |issn=1037-1397 |s2cid=143914456}} == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. So Senge's father was exiled and Takatomi Senge never managed to be high priest of the shrine. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. It was only in 1947 under the [[Allied Occupation of Japan]] that the Senge family managed to become high priests of Izumo Taisha again. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|[[Mokuyōkai]]}} a Japanese left wing political party. The party was sympathetic to the [[Japanese Communist Party]] and [[Junpei Gomikawa]] published through it.{{Cite journal |last=Ward |first=Vanessa B. |date=2006-09-01 |title=The spectre of the left: Iwanami shoten, ideology and publishing in early postwar Japan |url=https://doi.org/10.1080/10371390600883578 |journal=Japanese Studies |volume=26 |issue=2 |pages=171–184 |doi=10.1080/10371390600883578 |issn=1037-1397 |s2cid=143914456}} == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. So Senge's father was exiled and Takatomi Senge never managed to be high priest of the shrine. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. It was only in 1947 under the [[Allied Occupation of Japan]] that the Senge family managed to become high priests of Izumo Taisha again. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - [[尾崎洵盛]]（娘婿） | 国旗 = JPN | 職名 = 第14代 [[法務大臣|司法大臣]] | 内閣 = [[第1次西園寺内閣]] | 就任日 = [[1908年]][[3月25日]] | 退任日 = 1908年[[7月14日]] | 国旗2 = 東京都 | 職名2 = 第17代 [[東京都知事一覧|東京府知事]] | 就任日2 = [[1898年]][[11月12日]] | 退任日2 = 1908年3月25日 | 国旗3 = 静岡県 | 職名3 = 第4代 [[静岡県知事]] | 就任日3 = [[1897年]][[4月7日]] | 退任日3 = 1898年[[7月16日]] | 国旗4 = 埼玉県 | 職名4 = 第4代 [[埼玉県知事]] | 就任日4 = [[1894年]][[1月20日]] | 退任日4 = 1897年4月7日 | 国旗5 = JPN | 職名5 = [[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院議員]] | 選挙区5 = [[貴族院男爵議員]] | 当選回数5 = 4回 | 就任日5 = [[1890年]][[7月10日]] - 1897年[[7月9日]]
1897年7月10日 - [[1904年]]7月9日
1904年7月10日 - [[1911年]]7月9日
1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|{{ill|Mokuyōkai|ja|木曜会 (貴族院)}}}} a Japanese left wing political party. == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. So Senge's father was exiled and Takatomi Senge never managed to be high priest of the shrine. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. It was only in 1947 under the [[Allied Occupation of Japan]] that the Senge family managed to become high priests of Izumo Taisha again. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|{{ill|Mokuyōkai|ja|木曜会 (貴族院)}}}} a Japanese political faction dedicated to representing the interests of barons over higher nobility. == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. So Senge's father was exiled and Takatomi Senge never managed to be high priest of the shrine. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. It was only in 1947 under the [[Allied Occupation of Japan]] that the Senge family managed to become high priests of Izumo Taisha again. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|{{ill|Mokuyōkai|ja|木曜会 (貴族院)}}}} a Japanese political faction dedicated to representing the interests of barons over higher nobility. == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. So Senge's father was exiled and Takatomi Senge never managed to be high priest of the shrine. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. It was only in 1947 under the [[Allied Occupation of Japan]] that the Senge family managed to become high priests of Izumo Taisha again. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1897年7月10日 - [[1904年]]7月9日
1904年7月10日 - [[1911年]]7月9日
1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|{{ill|Mokuyōkai|ja|木曜会 (貴族院)}}}} a Japanese political faction dedicated to representing the interests of barons over higher nobility. == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. So Senge's father was exiled and Takatomi Senge never managed to be high priest of the shrine. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. It was only in 1947 under the [[Allied Occupation of Japan]] that the Senge family managed to become high priests of Izumo Taisha again. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation Izumo Kokuso of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - {{Short description|Japanese politician and religious leader}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{AfC submission|||ts=20230528185542|u=Immanuelle|ns=118}} {{Draft}} {{Afc comment|1=[[Takatomi Senge]] should link here[[User:FloridaArmy|FloridaArmy]] [[User_talk:FloridaArmy|talk]] 14:00, 27 May 2023 (UTC)}} * {{Infobox officeholder | 人名 = 千家 尊福 | 各国語表記 = せんげ たかとみ | 画像 = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg | 画像サイズ = 240px | 画像説明 = [[大礼服]]で[[正装]]した千家 | 国略称 = {{JPN}} | 生年月日 = [[1845年]][[9月7日]]
（[[弘化]]2年[[8月6日 (旧暦)|8月6日]]） | 出生地 = {{JPN}} [[出雲国]]
（現・[[島根県]]） | 没年月日 = {{death date|1918|1|3|1845|9|7}} | 前職 = [[神道大社教]]管長 | 所属政党 = （[[研究会 (貴族院)|研究会]]→）
[[木曜会 (貴族院)|木曜会]] | 称号・勲章 = [[正二位]]
[[勲一等旭日大綬章]]
[[男爵]] | 子女 = [[千家元麿]]（長男・[[庶子]]）
千家鯱丸（三男・庶子）
[[千家哲麿]]（六男） | 親族（政治家） = [[伏原宣明]]（義祖父）
[[千家尊澄]]（父）
[[千家尊統]]（婿養子）
[[千家紀彦]]（孫）
[[千家国麿]]（玄孫）
[[尾崎洵盛]]（娘婿） | 国旗 = JPN | 職名 = 第14代 [[法務大臣|司法大臣]] | 内閣 = [[第1次西園寺内閣]] | 就任日 = [[1908年]][[3月25日]] | 退任日 = 1908年[[7月14日]] | 国旗2 = 東京都 | 職名2 = 第17代 [[東京都知事一覧|東京府知事]] | 就任日2 = [[1898年]][[11月12日]] | 退任日2 = 1908年3月25日 | 国旗3 = 静岡県 | 職名3 = 第4代 [[静岡県知事]] | 就任日3 = [[1897年]][[4月7日]] | 退任日3 = 1898年[[7月16日]] | 国旗4 = 埼玉県 | 職名4 = 第4代 [[埼玉県知事]] | 就任日4 = [[1894年]][[1月20日]] | 退任日4 = 1897年4月7日 | 国旗5 = JPN | 職名5 = [[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院議員]] | 選挙区5 = [[貴族院男爵議員]] | 当選回数5 = 4回 | 就任日5 = [[1890年]][[7月10日]] - 1897年[[7月9日]]
1897年7月10日 - [[1904年]]7月9日
1904年7月10日 - [[1911年]]7月9日
1911年7月10日 | 退任日5 = 1918年1月3日 | image = Takatomi Senge 01.jpg }} Baron '''Takatomi Senge''' was a Japanaese priest and politician. He served as governor of two prefectures, [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 - 1908, and in the [[House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]]. He was an organizer of the 1907 [[Tokyo Industrial Exhibition]].https://books.google.com/books?id=j0BBAQAAMAAJ&pg=PA287&dq=Tokyo+Industrial+Exhibition+1907&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&source=gb_mobile_search&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiwqMn3rJP_AhXBRzABHVpGAqc4ChDoAXoECAQQAw#v=onepage&q=Tokyo%20Industrial%20Exhibition%201907&f=falsehttps://www.edohakuarchives.jp/detail-23749.html He was a Shinto Priest and the founder of [[Izumo-taishakyo]], and the 80th high priest of [[Izumo-taisha]]{{cite book |author=Miller, R.A. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M4ba4AeaFHAC |title=現代日本文読本: Graded Lessons for Mastering the Written Language |date=1962 |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=9780804816472 |page=115 |accessdate=2015-01-01}}{{cite book |author1=Teeuwen, M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yBFFAz4z3gUC |title=Shinto, a Short History |author2=Breen, J. |author3=Inoue, N. |author4=Mori, M. |date=2003 |publisher=RoutledgeCurzon |isbn=9780415311793 |page=177 |accessdate=2015-01-01}} (an eightieth generation descendant of the first {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}).https://archive.ph/wip/3YTSG He founded the {{Nihongo3|The Thursday Association|木曜会|{{ill|Mokuyōkai|ja|木曜会 (貴族院)}}}} a Japanese political faction dedicated to representing the interests of barons over higher nobility. == Ancestry == {{Main|Izumo-taisha}} The descendants of {{Nihongo|[[Amenohohi-no-mikoto]]|天穂日命}}, the second son of {{Nihongo|[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]]|天照大御神}}, the sun goddess whose first son is the ancestor of the imperial family, have been, in the name of {{Nihongo|Izumo Kokuso|出雲国造}} or governor of Izumo, taking over rituals because when Izumo-taisha was founded Amenohohi-no-mikoto rendered service to Okuninushi-no-kami. The family's conflict around 1340 made them separated into two lineages, {{Nihongo|Senge|千家}} and {{Nihongo|Kitajima|北島}}."Japantimes - Princess Noriko to wed" [http://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2014/05/27/national/princess-noriko-to-wed-son-of-izumo-taisha-chief-priest/#.VC_BNCjLZ5g], Tokyo, 27 May 2014. Retrieved on 4 October 2014 In 1871, Izumo-taisha was designated as an Imperial-associated shrine and the government sent a new administrator so Izumo kokuso families were no more the administrators of Izumo-taisha. So Senge's father was exiled and Takatomi Senge never managed to be high priest of the shrine. Senge reorganized the traditions of Izumo Taisha under the organization [[Izumo-taishakyo]] and was instrumental in the development of [[Sect Shinto]]. It was only in 1947 under the [[Allied Occupation of Japan]] that the Senge family managed to become high priests of Izumo Taisha again. {{Nihongo|Takatoshi Senge|千家尊祀}}, the 83rd-generation {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} of Senge lineage, was chosen to be the chief priest of Izumo-taisha in 1947. He died in February 2002 at the age of 89. == Political offices == [[ja:千家尊福]] [[simple:Takatomi Senge]] Senge held many political offices in his life. He was a member of Japan's House of Peers, a group of nobles who were like the British [[House of Lords]]. He was also the Governor of [[Saitama Prefecture]] from 1894 to 1897, the Governor of [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] from 1897 to 1898, the [[Governor of Tokyo]] from 1898 to 1908 and the [[Minister of Justice (Japan)|Minister of Justice]] in 1908. == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} * [[State Shinto]] * [[Izumo-taisha]] * [[Izumo-taishakyo]] * [[Sect Shinto]] * [[Noriko Senge]] [[fr:Senge Takatomi]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Founders of religions]] [[Category:Japanese Shintoists]] [[Category:1845 births]] [[Category:1918 deaths]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun]] [[Category:Kazoku]] [[Category:Government ministers of Japan]] [[Category:Governors of Tokyo]] [[Category:Governors of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Governors of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:People from Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Royal Victorian Order]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class]] [[Category:Grand Cordons of the Order of the Rising Sun]] }} == External links == * [http://www.law.kyushu-u.ac.jp/~shichinohe/minpo/(37)_senge_takatomi.htm 千家尊福 経歴] * [http://www.izumooyashiro.or.jp/hitobito/taisyakyo/index.html 出雲大社教] * {{Commons category-inline}} {{Clear}} {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} Chairman of the {{ill|Legal commission|ja|法律取調委員会}} | years = 1908 | before = {{ill|Matsuda Masahisa|ja|松田正久}} | after = {{ill|Okabe Nakamoto|ja|岡部長職}} }} {{S-bus}} {{Succession box | title = President of [[Tokyo Toden]] | years = 1909 - 1911 | before = {{ill|Gengaku Mutaguchi|ja|牟田口元学}} | after = Abolished }} {{S-other}} {{Succession box | title = Head of [[Izumo-taishakyo]] | years = [[1882]] - [[1888]] | before = Position established | after = Senge Sonai (千家尊愛) }} {{Succession box | title = Head Priest of [[Izumo Taisha]] | years = [[1872]] - 1882 | before = Takazumi Senge (千家尊澄) | after = Senge Takaki (千家尊紀) }} {{Succession box | title = 80th {{ill|Izumo no kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}} | years = 1872 - 1882 | before = Takazumi Senge (千家尊澄) | after = Senge Takaki (千家尊紀) }} {{S-reg|jp}} {{Succession box | title = Baron | years = [[1884]] -
